data.content:
I can see you have an MSTest project with a failing test — `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects `15.00` but the method returns `10.00` for express.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically when you save changes:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user has a .NET test project with MSTest. They want to use hot reload to iterate faster on failing tests. I can set up `dotnet watch test` which will automatically re-run tests when files change.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so they can iterate on the code.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-18ab49ea558d45629715dc2d01901fa4 && dotnet watch test --no-restore 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test with hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.reasoningText:
There seems to be an issue with `dotnet watch`. The error says "Error(s) finding watch items project file 'TestProject.csproj'". This could be because the project doesn't have watch-related properties. Let me check if this is a known issue and try a different approach.

Let me first try running `dotnet test` to see if it works at all, and then investigate the watch issue.

data.content: The project has a compilation error — missing `using System;`. Let me fix that and also check the target framework, then set up watch:
data.reasoningText:
There's a compilation error - `ArgumentException` needs `using System;`. Also the project is targeting `net8.0` but the SDK installed is 10.0. Let me fix the compilation error first by adding `using System;`, then set up `dotnet watch test`.

Actually, let me also check the TargetFramework. The SDK is 10.0 but the project targets net8.0. Let me check if net8.0 runtime is available.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running. Here's the status:

- ✅ **Passed:** `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5`
- ❌ **Failed:** `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expects `15.00` but gets `10.00`

The watcher is live and waiting for file changes. Whenever you save `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`, it will automatically rebuild and re-run the tests. 

The fix for the failing test is on **line 23** — change `"express" => 10.00m` to `"express" => 15.00m`. Want me to fix it?
